reason: 実行時ID is a distinct log output item ("処理の実行を一意に識別するID") that is entirely absent from the s1 output items table. Developers consulting this knowledge file to understand what HTTPアクセスログ outputs will receive an incomplete list, missing an item that appears in actual log output (visible as the execution ID bracket in the log example). This is a factual gap in reference documentation used for log configuration and analysis. The hints_missing finding is a secondary consequence of the same omission. Together they constitute medium user impact.

findings_assessment.location: s1 — HTTPアクセスログの出力項目 table
findings_assessment.justification: 実行時ID is a named log output item defined in the source RST between リクエストID and ユーザID. Its absence means an agent answering 'What items does HTTPアクセスログ output?' or 'What is 実行時ID?' will give an incomplete or wrong answer. In enterprise log configuration and audit trail analysis, knowing every available log field matters — especially since 実行時ID (execution-time ID) is conceptually distinct from リクエストID (request ID) and the two can appear together in the same log line. Not blocking (other items and configuration details are correct), but a concrete factual gap. Phase E did not fix this: the s1 section content in the Phase E output is byte-for-byte identical to the original, indicating the fix agent regenerated the unchanged file rather than applying the targeted row insertion.

Absence of '実行時ID' from the hints array means this file may not be retrieved when a user queries specifically about 実行時ID. However, since the content itself is also missing (finding 1), adding the hint alone would not resolve the underlying problem. The impact is therefore lower than finding 1 — it compounds the retrieval gap but is not independently actionable. Phase E did not fix this either, for the same reason: the hints array in the Phase E output is identical to the original.
